repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 has shared-completion cellwise nonsharpness for ADE, not a K-support direct/indirect random-set frontier

published_prior_art.cite: SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026: sharp bounds for fixed canonical exposure misspecification, not shared sparse latent K-label support gaps

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy/v1 studies nonrectangular mediation geometry; pid_network_exposure/v1 studies ADE hidden-edge compatibility, neither gives Delta_K for network direct/indirect vectors

published_prior_art.cite: HuLiWager2022 gives direct/indirect decomposition under specified exposure structure; no latent-map nonrectangular sharp-set result

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 2 already proposes capacitated incidence certificates; this narrows to K=2 threshold/OR endpoint witnesses and active bases

published_prior_art.cite: EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965 and Wolsey1975 supply polyhedral ingredients, not the causal endpoint-witness certificate

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 2 is the closest certificate precedent but only for fixed hidden-edge exposure ADE endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 gives random-set support characterization; EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965/Wolsey1975 give generic discrete-optimization machinery

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Make Conjecture 3 visibly subordinate to Conjectures 1-2 by calling it an audit/certificate theorem, so the flagship kernel remains Delta_K/Gamma_K rather than generic MILP certificate existence.
Add a one-line computational contract for evaluating h_K and h_cell,K at the witness, including finite enumeration size and the Charnes-Cooper endpoint program dimensions.
If Stage 0 confirms the 1/40 witness, add a corollary that explicitly separates SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026-style marginal exposure bounds from the joint sparse-map support region.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 on Conjectures 1-2 first; treat Conjectures 3-4 as certificate support rather than the primary novelty kernel.
